---
title: "Larval Growth & Caste Determination"
subtitle: "Micronest time-lapse experiment"
author: "Kendrick"
date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"

# -- Page behavior --
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
number-sections: true
code-fold: true          # all code chunks collapsed by default; reader can expand
code-tools: true         # adds a </> button to show/hide all code at once
fig-align: center
execute:
  freeze: true           # only re-run when you explicitly render this page
  warning: false

# -- Lightbox for zoomable images --
lightbox: true
---

<!-- ============================================================
     This file is a feature showcase, not real content.
     Replace placeholder text/code with your actual work.
     ============================================================ -->

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Reproducibility Info
**Last rendered:** {{< meta date >}} |
**Source:** [`larval-growth.qmd`](https://github.com/soulsynapse/antscihub/blob/main/lab-notebooks/larval-growth.qmd) |
**Render path:** GitHub Actions builds and deploys the site from `main`
:::


::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview

### Background

Write your project motivation here. Quarto's citation system uses standard
BibTeX. Once a bibliography file is added, cite inline with `[@key]` and the
bibliography will render automatically. For example: worker-larva interactions
can mediate caste determination through nutritional and pheromonal signals.

### Experimental Design

Quarto renders Mermaid diagrams natively -- no external tool, no image export:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-design
%%| fig-cap: "Experimental design: 128 micronests, queenright vs. queenless."
%%| fig-width: 6

flowchart LR
    A[Source colonies<br/>n = 8] --> B{Random assignment}
    B --> C[Queenright<br/>64 micronests]
    B --> D[Queenless<br/>64 micronests]
    C --> E[1 larva + 5 workers<br/>per micronest]
    D --> E
    E --> F[Time-lapse imaging<br/>1 photo/hr]
```

You can cross-reference this anywhere in the document with `@fig-design`.
See @fig-design for the full layout.


### Key Parameters

Quarto tables support cross-referencing and caption formatting:

| Parameter | Value | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Micronests | 128 | 64 per treatment |
| Workers per nest | 5 | from source colony |
| Larvae per nest | 1 | 1st instar at start |
| Imaging interval | 1 hr | rpicam-still |
| Duration | 30 days | or until eclosion |

: Experimental parameters for the micronest growth assay. {#tbl-params}

Reference it elsewhere: see @tbl-params for the full parameter set.


## Data

### Accessing Raw Images

Your images live on AntSciHub. Since the CDN uses structured URLs, you can
link directly:

```
https://antscihub.org/v/{colony_id}/{nest_id}/{timestamp}
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Quarto feature: callout blocks
These come in five flavors -- `note`, `tip`, `warning`, `caution`, `important`.
They can be collapsible (`collapse="true"`) and support full markdown inside.
:::


### Loading & Summarizing Data

Code chunks execute at render time. `code-fold: true` in the YAML means the
code is hidden by default but expandable -- readers see the output, researchers
can inspect the code.

```{python}
#| label: fig-growth-trajectories
#| fig-cap: "Simulated larval growth trajectories by treatment. Replace with real data."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

np.random.seed(42)
days = np.arange(0, 30)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))

# Simulated trajectories -- replace with actual DB query
for i in range(12):
    treatment = "queenright" if i < 6 else "queenless"
    color = "#2d6a4f" if treatment == "queenright" else "#d4a373"
    k = np.random.uniform(0.08, 0.15)
    L_inf = np.random.uniform(2.5, 4.0) if treatment == "queenright" else np.random.uniform(1.8, 3.0)
    noise = np.random.normal(0, 0.05, len(days))
    length = L_inf * (1 - np.exp(-k * days)) + noise
    ax.plot(days, length, color=color, alpha=0.6, linewidth=1.5,
            label=treatment if i in [0, 6] else None)

ax.set_xlabel("Day")
ax.set_ylabel("Body length (mm)")
ax.legend(frameon=False)
ax.spines[['top', 'right']].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

Cross-reference: the growth trajectories in @fig-growth-trajectories suggest
treatment effects on asymptotic size.


### Inline Computed Values

Quarto can embed computed values directly in prose:

```{python}
#| echo: false
n_total = 128
n_per_treatment = 64
imaging_days = 30
total_images = n_total * imaging_days * 24
```

The experiment will generate approximately `{python} f"{total_images:,}"`
images over `{python} imaging_days` days -- this number updates automatically
if you change the parameters above.


## Analysis

### Growth Model

Quarto renders LaTeX natively. Useful for documenting your statistical models
alongside the code that fits them:

Individual growth follows a von Bertalanffy curve:

$$
L(t) = L_\infty \left(1 - e^{-k(t - t_0)}\right)
$$ {#eq-vonbert}

where $L_\infty$ is the asymptotic length, $k$ is the growth rate constant,
and $t_0$ is the theoretical age at zero length. See @eq-vonbert.

The treatment effect enters through $L_\infty$:

$$
L_{\infty,i} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot \text{queenless}_i + u_{\text{colony}(i)}
$$ {#eq-treatment}

with colony as a random intercept (@eq-treatment). This is a mixed-effects
nonlinear regression -- `nlme::nlme` or `scipy.optimize.curve_fit` per
individual followed by a linear model on the extracted parameters.


### Fitted Results

```{python}
#| label: fig-parameter-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Comparison of fitted growth parameters between treatments."
#| fig-subcap:
#|   - "Asymptotic length"
#|   - "Growth rate constant"
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-width: 4
#| fig-height: 4

# --- Quarto feature: sub-figures with shared caption and layout control ---

# Panel A: L_inf by treatment
fig1, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(4, 4))
qr_linf = np.random.normal(3.2, 0.3, 64)
ql_linf = np.random.normal(2.4, 0.4, 64)
parts = ax1.violinplot([qr_linf, ql_linf], positions=[1, 2], showmedians=True)
for pc in parts['bodies']:
    pc.set_alpha(0.6)
ax1.set_xticks([1, 2])
ax1.set_xticklabels(["Queenright", "Queenless"])
ax1.set_ylabel("$L_\\infty$ (mm)")
ax1.spines[['top', 'right']].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Panel B: k by treatment
fig2, ax2 = plt.subplots(figsize=(4, 4))
qr_k = np.random.normal(0.12, 0.02, 64)
ql_k = np.random.normal(0.10, 0.025, 64)
parts = ax2.violinplot([qr_k, ql_k], positions=[1, 2], showmedians=True)
for pc in parts['bodies']:
    pc.set_alpha(0.6)
ax2.set_xticks([1, 2])
ax2.set_xticklabels(["Queenright", "Queenless"])
ax2.set_ylabel("$k$ (day$^{-1}$)")
ax2.spines[['top', 'right']].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

Reference sub-figures individually: @fig-parameter-comparison-1 shows the
asymptotic length distributions, while @fig-parameter-comparison-2 shows
growth rate constants.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Quarto feature: margin notes
You can place content in the page margin with the `.column-margin` class.
Useful for side-commentary, small reference figures, or annotation without
interrupting the main flow. Add `column: margin` to a code chunk's YAML
to push its output to the margin.
:::


## Methods

### CV Pipeline

Describe your SAM-based segmentation, morphometric extraction, etc.

::: {.column-margin}
**Pipeline version:** `v0.3.2` \
**Last validated:** 2026-06-15
:::

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-pipeline
%%| fig-cap: "Image processing pipeline from raw frame to morphometric measurement."

flowchart TD
    A[Raw frame<br/>RPi camera] --> B[Background subtraction]
    B --> C[SAM segmentation]
    C --> D{Mask QC}
    D -->|pass| E[Morphometric extraction]
    D -->|fail| F[Manual correction tool]
    F --> E
    E --> G[Spline-based length]
    E --> H[Ellipse-based area]
    G --> I[(Measurements DB)]
    H --> I
```


### Protocol Details

::: {.callout-caution collapse="true"}
## Quarto feature: collapsible callouts
Lengthy protocols, equipment lists, or troubleshooting notes can go inside
collapsible callouts. The reader sees the header; they expand only if needed.
This keeps the page scannable without losing detail.

**Example protocol step:** Aspirate 5 workers from source colony using a
modified 15 mL Falcon tube aspirator. Transfer to micronest arena within 30
seconds to minimize stress handling. Confirm 5 workers visually before sealing.
:::


### Linking to AntSciHub

Reference specific video observations inline:

> The larval secretion event in colony 4, nest 17 was first observed at
> hour 342. See the raw footage:
> [AntSciHub link](https://antscihub.org/v/colony04/nest17/t342).


:::

<!-- ============================================================
     END OF TABSET
     ============================================================ -->


## References {.unnumbered}

Add `bibliography: references.bib` to the YAML and cite entries with `[@key]`
once this notebook starts using literature references.
